chunk_summary: Donor eligibility screening ensures safe milk donation by assessing health history, conducting serologic tests, and requiring periodic reassessment to prevent infection transmission and harmful exposures.
learning_objectives: Understand the purpose and process of donor eligibility screening for milk banking.
Identify exclusion criteria and temporary deferral conditions for milk donors.
Learn the serologic testing requirements and periodic reassessment protocols for ongoing donors.
Recognize the importance of hygienic milk expression and cold-chain transport.
key_facts: Donor screening includes health history and serologic testing for HIV-1/2, HBsAg, HCV, and syphilis (VDRL or RPR).
Temporary deferral applies to acute febrile illness, mastitis, or short-term medication use incompatible with infant feeding.
Periodic reassessment is recommended every 3–6 months during active donation.
Donors must provide informed consent for testing and anonymous, non-commercial milk use.
Milk expressed during temporary deferral periods must not enter the donation pool.
reference_quote: Donor eligibility screening aims to prevent transmission of infections and harmful exposures through donated milk.
